title: RepoBrief practical utility hardening
goal: Turn RepoBrief/rLens from a strong evidence factory into a default-useful agent context system by registering the practical query, citation, freshness, context, review-coverage, semantic, federation and runtime-evidence gaps without moving mutation authority into RepoBrief core.
completion: All Lenskit/RepoBrief/rLens practical utility gaps identified in the max-dump critique and Lenskit PR #911 are mapped to Bureau tasks or explicitly deduplicated against existing RBV1/RBAE/BUR tasks.
High-leverage near-term tasks for resolved evidence query, live-repo citations and freshness registry are scheduled before broader MCP or rename work.
Every task preserves RepoBrief boundaries: no hidden refresh on reads, no Git mutation in RepoBrief core, no patch/test/shell authority, no review or runtime correctness verdict.

source_threads: Lenskit PR #911 local task registration follow-up
metadata.completion_ready_reason: All twenty-one registered RPU tasks are verified; Doctor independently recommends completion-ready with zero open tasks.
